threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the Tube' is the best way to get around. Not realizing the Overground is your new best friend.
Caught a bloke singing opera on the 355 last night. Crowds went from groaning to cheering in seconds. Brixton's night life never disappoints.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a street musician playing accordion while a couple argued about their cat on the corner. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a late-night Döner while debating if the U-Bahn will actually run on time during a strike. It's a wild mix of chaos and comfort.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they know to avoid Civic Center when BART’s down; tourists just stand around confused while we pass them by.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a street performer using the Muni bus stop as a stage while juggling flaming torches. Only in the Tenderloin would that be a Tuesday vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but locals know it’s the little corner bars and nightly jam sessions that give it life.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they ask for a 'café con leche' instead of just a 'café'. It’s the little things that give them away.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Tube' instead of 'the underground'—dead giveaway you're a newbie.
Caught a guy doing yoga in the back of the 196 last night, complete with a mini incense burner. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids using an old fridge as a makeshift BBQ on the street while an elderly lady watched with a smile. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find converted factories serving up brunch alongside graffiti artists creating masterpieces on Sunday mornings.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're locals when they dodge those random BART evacuations like it's a dance move. Tourists just freeze, wide-eyed.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5-Fulton this morning, and a guy tried to pay with a crumpled fortune cookie slip. Muni drivers aren't even flinching anymore.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the vibrant street art is a recent fad. In Gràcia, those murals are like a living history, telling stories of protests and celebrations.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at Gràcia's roundabouts—locals zip through while newcomers hesitate, checking maps nervously.
